scope.note: Codex-only identity cleanup prep for global route records. No corridor aliases were needed; no entity, project, or treatment_component records are in scope.

proposed_aliases.reason: Both records represent the same global M34 local bus route on 34th Street. The alias and target share route_id M34, route_type_normalized local, and service_variant local; source evidence references M34 station/route context with no SBS, express, or limited-stop variant conflict.

evidence.quote: hybrid- electric, low floor buses currently used on M34 and M16 routes

proposed_aliases.reason: Both records represent the same global M34 local bus route on 34th Street. The alias and target share route_id M34, route_type_normalized local, and service_variant local; the alias display name explicitly says M34 Bus Route (Local).

evidence.quote: hybrid- electric, low floor buses currently used on M34 and M16 routes

proposed_aliases.reason: Both records represent the same global M34 local bus route. The alias and target share route_id M34, route_type_normalized local, and service_variant local; source evidence discusses M34 planned improvements and M34/M16 buses without indicating a different route variant.

evidence.quote: Off-board fare collection on M34 and M16
hybrid- electric, low floor buses currently used on M34 and M16 routes

proposed_aliases.reason: Both records represent the same global Bx41 Limited route on Webster Avenue. The alias and target share route_id Bx41, route_type_normalized limited_stop, and service_variant limited_stop; the source block says Bx41 SBS replaces the Bx41 Limited while preserving Bx41 local as separate.

evidence.quote: Bx41 SBS replaces the Bx41 Limited
comparing them to the current Bx41 Limited stops

proposed_aliases.reason: Both records represent the same global Bx41 Limited route on Webster Avenue. The alias and target share route_id Bx41, route_type_normalized limited_stop, and service_variant limited_stop; source evidence explicitly lists Bx41 LTD and distinguishes it from Bx41 Local.

evidence.quote: comparing them to the current Bx41 Limited stops

proposed_aliases.reason: Both records represent the same global M1 local bus route in Madison Avenue study-area material. The alias and target share route_id M1, route_type_normalized local, and service_variant local; both evidence blocks list M1 among local buses in the same East 23rd to East 42nd Street study area.

evidence.quote: M1, M2, M3, M4, Q32 local buses
M1, M2, M3, M4, Q32 local buses

proposed_aliases.reason: Both records represent the same global M1 local bus route and cite the same Madison Avenue CB5 May 2025 source block. The alias and target share route_id M1, route_type_normalized local, service_variant local, and primary source.

evidence.quote: M1, M2, M3, M4, Q32 local buses
M1, M2, M3, M4, Q32 local buses

proposed_aliases.reason: Both records represent the same global BM4 express route in Church Avenue material. The alias and target share route_id BM4, route_type_normalized express, and service_variant express; both source blocks cite BM3 & BM4 express buses with direct Ocean Avenue service.

record_existence_and_kind.command: sqlite3 -header -csv data/canonical.db "select r.record_id,r.record_kind,r.display_name,rt.route_id,rt.route_type_normalized,json_extract(r.payload,'$.service_variant') as service_variant,r.primary_source_id from records r join routes rt on rt.record_id=r.record_id where r.record_id in (<selected aliases and targets>) order by rt.route_id,r.record_id;"
record_existence_and_kind.result: Returned all 12 selected alias/target records as record_kind=route. M34, M1 records are local/local; Bx41 records are limited_stop/limited_stop; BM4 records are express/express.

existing_alias_conflicts.identity_aliases_command: sqlite3 -header -csv data/canonical.db "select kind,alias,target from identity_aliases where kind in ('route','corridor') and alias in (<selected aliases and targets>);"
existing_alias_conflicts.merges_json_command: jq -r --argjson ids '[<selected aliases and targets>]' '.aliases | to_entries[] | .key as $kind | .value | to_entries[] | select(.key as $k | ($ids | index($k))) | [$kind,.key,.value] | @tsv' data/identity-overrides/merges.json

do_not_merge_conflicts.db_command: sqlite3 -header -csv data/canonical.db "select kind,record_id_a,record_id_b,reason from do_not_merge where kind='route' and (record_id_a in (<selected aliases and targets>) or record_id_b in (<selected aliases and targets>));"
do_not_merge_conflicts.json_command: jq -r --argjson ids '[<selected aliases and targets>]' '.pairs | to_entries[] | .key as $kind | .value[] | select(any(.record_ids[]; . as $rid | ($ids | index($rid)))) | [$kind,.record_ids[0],.record_ids[1],.reason] | @tsv' data/identity-overrides/do-not-merge.json

prior_prep_conflicts.command: jq -r --argjson ids '[<selected aliases>]' '.proposed_aliases[]? | select((.kind=="route" or .kind=="corridor") and ($ids | index(.alias_id))) | [.kind,.alias_id,.target_id] | @tsv' data/review_notes/codex-identity-batch0{47..99}-prep.json 2>/dev/null | sort -u
prior_prep_conflicts.result: Returned no rows; none of the selected alias_ids appeared in prior Codex identity prep artifacts through identity099.

scope_and_variant_checks.command: sqlite3 -header -csv data/canonical.db "select rt.route_id, rt.route_type_normalized, coalesce(json_extract(r.payload,'$.service_variant'),''), count(*) n, group_concat(r.record_id,' | ') ids from records r join routes rt on rt.record_id=r.record_id where r.record_kind='route' and rt.route_id is not null group by rt.route_id, rt.route_type_normalized, coalesce(json_extract(r.payload,'$.service_variant'),'') having n>1 order by n desc, rt.route_id limit 120;"
scope_and_variant_checks.result: Selected only clusters with identical route_id and compatible normalized service variant/type: M34 local, Bx41 limited_stop, M1 local, and BM4 express. No corridors were proposed, so no corridor-limit ambiguity is introduced.

source_evidence_commands.command: sqlite3 -header -csv data/canonical.db "select e.record_id,e.source_id,e.block_id,e.page_number,json_extract(e.ref_json,'$.source_quote') as source_quote from evidence_refs e where e.record_id in (<selected aliases and targets>) order by e.record_id,e.ordinal;"
source_evidence_commands.result: Confirmed citeable evidence refs exist for every selected alias and target record.

source_evidence_commands.command: sqlite3 -header -csv data/canonical.db "select source_id,block_id,substr(replace(replace(raw_text,char(10),' '),char(13),' '),1,260) as quote from blocks_fts where (<selected evidence refs>) order by source_id,block_id;"
source_evidence_commands.result: Confirmed source blocks explicitly mention M34, Bx41 Limited/LTD, M1 local buses, and BM4 express buses used in the proposed aliases.

candidate_rejection_notes: Dropped B69 candidates after inspection because one candidate's citeable source block was a B41 photo despite the route record's B69 description; retained only records with more direct route evidence.
Did not include corridor candidates; route-only aliases provided a full high-confidence batch and avoided corridor limit/scope ambiguity.
